---
title: "Median Age by Neighbourhood"
description: "Census-tract median age across Canada's cities, with each neighbourhood's age breakdown — 2021 Census"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.config import get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map
```

The median age of each **census tract** — roughly a neighbourhood of 2,500–8,000
people — from the 2021 Census. **Zoom and pan to your own area**, and hover for the
neighbourhood's full age mix (children, working age, seniors) and population. Census
tracts cover cities and larger towns; sparsely populated rural areas aren't divided
into tracts, so they appear blank.

The contrasts are stark: tracts around universities have median ages in the low 20s,
new outer suburbs cluster in the early 30s as young families move where homes are
larger and cheaper, and some established or retirement communities have median ages
above 60. Compare this map with the
[median household income](../income/neighbourhoods.qmd) and
[home value](../housing/neighbourhoods.qmd) maps — they share the same census
tracts, so you can flip between who lives in a neighbourhood and what living there
costs.

This detailed view lives on its own page (≈3 MB of boundary data) so the main
[Population & Growth](index.qmd) page stays fast to load.

[← Back to Population & Growth](index.qmd)

```{python}
#| echo: false
geo_path = "data/geo/ct_2021.geojson"
ct_age_path = "data/geo/statcan_ct_age_2021.csv"
if os.path.exists(geo_path) and os.path.exists(ct_age_path):
    ct_geo = json.load(open(geo_path))
    ct_df = pd.read_csv(ct_age_path, dtype={"ctuid": str})
    ct_df["pop_fmt"] = ct_df["population"].map(
        lambda v: f"{v:,.0f}" if pd.notna(v) else "n/a")
    # Cap the colour range at the 2nd–98th percentile: a handful of student-housing
    # (~22) and retirement/long-term-care (~85) tracts would otherwise stretch the
    # scale and flatten typical neighbourhoods. Hover always shows the true value.
    zlo = float(ct_df["median_age"].quantile(0.02))
    zhi = float(ct_df["median_age"].quantile(0.98))
    fig = choropleth_map(ct_geo, ct_df, "ctuid", "median_age", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Median age", value_fmt=".1f", value_suffix=" yrs",
        colorscale="Viridis", zmin=zlo, zmax=zhi, height=720,
        extra_hover=[("0–14", "share_0_14", ".0f", "%"),
                     ("15–64", "share_15_64", ".0f", "%"),
                     ("65+", "share_65_plus", ".0f", "%"),
                     ("Population", "pop_fmt", "", "")],
        source_note="Source: 2021 Census of Population (Statistics Canada, Census Profile 98-401-X2021007); boundaries from StatCan. Colour scale capped at the 2nd–98th percentile.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Census-tract age map not available — run `python -m pipeline.build_census_geo`.")
```

[← Back to Population & Growth](index.qmd)
